message: Hey everyone. I'm pumped today about our flash sale
You know it's been 56 years since the Beatles released Abbey Road right? My favourite album
56 years already?? Wow that makes me feel old...
Indeed! We're celebrating today by promoting a flash sale on retro items from 1969

Welcome to Bezs Beatles Day, remember communication is key to diagnose the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
We're a step closer to allowing folks to pay for items from the 1960s with Bitcoin thanks to Daniel!
Think of it like time travelling!
Wow good work Daniel! Looking forward to seeing it in action in prod
How's the test-driven development going?
TDDs going well. We are as solid as a rock! Though don't ask me what the Liskov Substitution Principle is...
erm - funny you should say that Daniel, I'm just testing my checkout service change in prod and I can't complete the payment - something's up
I hope my checkout service change is all good. Can someone check its not just me? And before you ask, yes I've cleared my cookies
Sorry Daniel it's not looking good on my end either
Uh oh...here we go again
@kdanjo-bloomberg.net can you "Size up the Issue" please and keep us informed
From what I can see it looks like we are still getting orders through
Get this sorted ASAP!!!
I get an Payment Unavailable error
:star: Reproduces the incident
Just got a message from my cousin in London, she can't buy any of the new Beatles merch either. Says her payment failed.
I'm noticing the failures seem concentrated in Europe. Looks like the UK is particularly affected.
How are the orders going through without payment. Is it only affecting some orders
I will start checking the logs now
Could it be your checkout change again @Shay? It broke the platform last week!
Can you route Europe orders through other country gateways
The checkout service logs are healthy and the service is working as intended
I see 504 HTTP errors in the payment service, which means the application is failing to serve the requests
Order completion rates are down for UK transactions, but other regions seem unaffected and are processing normally.
The 504 errors from the payment service are only appearing for UK transaction IDs, which aligns with the failed payments.
Can you bounce the payment service without affecting other regions
Rerouting would require a config change and a redeploy, but the underlying payment service latency issue would still need to be addressed.
Restarting the payment service is possible and should be safe since resource usage is normal, but the elevated latency and 504s are only impacting UK transactions.
Can the UK instance for the service be bounced
We can bounce just the UK instance of the payment service since it's isolated, and resource usage is within normal limits.
Go for it.
I'm initiating a restart of the UK payment service instance now and will monitor its health checks closely.
Keep an eye on the order traffic
I'm monitoring order volumes and will report any changes in UK transaction success rates after the restart.
Which other regions are placing orders at the moment and where is it working as expected
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Order traffic from the US, Germany, and France is steady and those regions are processing payments successfully.
Is the restart complete
Whats the ETA
:star: Manage actions and timings
The UK payment service instance has restarted and is now passing health checks, but I'll keep monitoring for any further latency spikes.
The UK payment service is stable post-restart, so if the trend holds, we should see normal transaction flow within a few minutes.
Let me try and reproduce again. Should i try to reload the webpage before trying again
Yes, please reload the page to ensure you're not seeing a cached error before attempting the payment again.
Still getting the same error
Despite the restart, the payment service health check in the UK is still returning 504s and latency remains high.
I'm still seeing 504 errors in the payment service logs for UK transactions after the restart.
Lets route the orders via Germany or France
I'll update the routing configuration to direct UK orders through the Germany and France payment gateways and redeploy the changes.
How long is that expected to take
The routing config update and redeploy should take about five to ten minutes to complete.
Are we seeing any successful orders in UK at the moment
Currently, there are no successful UK orders coming through since the issue began.
Have we tried bouncing other related services
So far, only the UK payment service instance has been restarted; no other related services have been bounced yet.
Can we put in a banner notifying UK users that we are expreiencing an issue
I recommend adding a temporary banner for UK users to inform them of the ongoing payment issue while we work on a fix.
Lets bounce checkout service
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
I'm restarting the UK checkout service instance now and will monitor its health status closely.
who is working on the banner
I'm coordinating with the frontend team to implement the UK-specific incident banner now.
Whats the ETA for restart
The UK checkout service restart should complete within the next two to three minutes, and I'll confirm once it's healthy.
Anything different for the UK instance of the services?
The 504 errors are only affecting UK transaction IDs, and this pattern started right after the recent deployment.
Any errors being seen in the logs for these servcies so far
Checkout service logs look clean and healthy so far, but the Payment service is returning 504 errors upstream.
roll back the last deployment
I will rollback my checkout change right away
Rollback complete!
Hmm, customer reports are still coming in at the same rate
The failure pattern hasn't changed - still 100% failure in the UK
Something else must be causing this
We need this fixed!
still the same error
Metrics show the UK payment failures are still at 100% even after the rollback.
Since Shay's rollback didn't help, maybe we should look at my change, but my code was thoroughly tested and shouldn't affect this.
Do we need another bounce after the rollback
Lets bounce again
A bounce after the rollback likely won't help since the issue persisted through the last restart, but I can do it if you want.
I'm restarting the UK checkout and payment services again now and will report back once they're up.
Any other recent rollouts?

Drill Wrap-Up

Thanks for playing! This session is now closed.

Please return to your Uptime Labs tab to review your performance and complete the Retrospective & Feedback form.

If you've already closed the tab, you can reopen the form using the button below.
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Yes, AdService and PaymentDatabase were both recently deployed to production as part of scheduled updates.
rollback those deployments too

Thanks for playing! Please complete your feedback here:
https://learn.uptimelabs.io/scenario/online-boutique/levels/5/7618/feedback
Okay, rolling back AdService now
Rollback complete. This change was just cosmetic, so it's unlikely to be the cause.
